architectural_decisions.title: Storage Technology Selection
architectural_decisions.context: COMP-001 (TaskStorageManager) is responsible for creating, appending, reading, updating, and deleting task records (RESP-001 to RESP-005). The system requires persistent storage within the Core Domain (DOM-001).
architectural_decisions.decision: Implement a file-based storage system using a single JSON file.
alternatives: SQLite database for structured querying and concurrency.
CSV file for simple parsing and smaller footprint.
architectural_decisions.rationale: A JSON file provides a balance between simplicity, human readability, and structured data capabilities without requiring external database dependencies. This aligns with the CLI nature of the application.
architectural_decisions.consequences: Positive: Minimal setup, no external dependencies. Negative: Limited concurrency support, manual schema management required.

architectural_decisions.title: Task Record Data Schema
architectural_decisions.context: COMP-001 must define the structure for task records upon creation (RESP-001) and when appending new records (RESP-002). The system must track status and task details.
architectural_decisions.decision: Define a JSON object schema for each task record containing: id, command, status, timestamp, and metadata.
alternatives: Flat text file with fixed-width columns.
Nested JSON structure with separate files per task.
architectural_decisions.rationale: A flat JSON array within a single file is optimal for the read-all (RESP-003) and delete (RESP-005) operations. It simplifies parsing logic for COMP-003.
architectural_decisions.consequences: Positive: Easy to inspect and debug storage content. Negative: File size grows linearly with task count, potentially impacting read performance on large datasets.

architectural_decisions.title: Concurrency and Execution Model
architectural_decisions.context: The application is a CLI tool (DOM-001) where COMP-002 (TaskCommandParser) controls flow and COMP-003 (TaskOutputWriter) writes data. Execution must be sequential to ensure data integrity in the single storage file.
architectural_decisions.decision: Adopt a single-threaded execution model for the entire application lifecycle.
alternatives: Multi-threaded execution for parallel task processing.
architectural_decisions.rationale: Given the file-based storage (ADR-001) and CLI nature, single-threaded execution prevents race conditions during file writes/reads (RESP-002, RESP-004) and simplifies error handling.
architectural_decisions.consequences: Positive: Simplified logic, no race conditions. Negative: No parallel processing capability, tasks must be processed sequentially.

architectural_decisions.title: Output Formatting Strategy
architectural_decisions.context: COMP-003 (TaskOutputWriter) is responsible for outputting stored task records to standard output (RESP-007). The output must be readable by the user in a terminal environment.
architectural_decisions.decision: Format output as a structured text representation (e.g., JSONL or formatted table) rather than raw binary or HTML.
architectural_decisions.rationale: Structured text (JSONL) ensures machine parsability if needed, while formatted tables improve human readability in a terminal. This satisfies RESP-007 without requiring external rendering libraries.
architectural_decisions.consequences: Positive: Compatible with standard CLI tools and log parsers. Negative: Less rich styling compared to HTML or Markdown.
